definition_origin: App Store and marketing assets are the collection of fixed-format graphics required to distribute and promote a mobile or web app — App Store Connect screenshots and preview videos, Google Play screenshots + feature graphic, web Open Graph + Twitter Card images, LinkedIn share images, and the app's marketing landing page hero. Format originates in print catalog conventions (the 'sales sheet' for retail), evolved through web banner ads (728x90 etc), and crystallized in 2008-2010 with the App Store's launch — the screenshot-as-billboard format. ASO (App Store Optimization) emerged ~2013 as a discipline parallel to SEO. 2026 reality: Apple now lets you upload only one or two screenshot sizes (6.9' or 6.5') and auto-scales for older devices; Google Play remains stricter; OG images have grown from afterthought to a product's most-shared visual asset.
core_purpose: **Visceral**: the App Store screenshots are the single most-viewed marketing asset for a mobile app — 90% of users do not scroll past the third screenshot. Google Play feature graphic is the banner across the listing. OG images dominate every Twitter/Slack/iMessage share. **Behavioral**: screenshots have a job-to-be-done — convey the value prop in 0.8 seconds at thumbnail size, then in ~3 seconds at full size. ASO research shows optimized screenshots can lift conversion by 24-30%. **Reflective**: returning users recognize a brand by its screenshot system; press articles re-use OG images; social shares amplify when the OG image is intentional. For our user's archetype mix: marketing assets are where archetype gets aggressive — Magician/Creator demands a hero illustration with depth; Caregiver demands warm faces and softness; Jester/Outlaw demands a contrarian headline. The one screenshot that costs the most attention to make is screenshot #1 — it's both the search-result thumbnail AND the first viewport, so it carries 50% of the conversion load.
when_needed: **Mandatory**: any app submitted to App Store Connect (iOS), Google Play, Mac App Store, Apple TV App Store, watchOS App Store. **Mandatory**: any web app that will be shared via social/Slack/email — without an OG image, link previews look broken. **Strongly recommended**: a Twitter card image (often the same as OG), LinkedIn share image (1200x627). **Optional but high-leverage**: TikTok/Instagram square (1080x1080), YouTube thumbnail (1280x720) if you're doing video marketing, podcast cover (3000x3000) if podcast launch. For the user's portfolio mix: consumer mobile = full asset set mandatory; B2B/dev tool = mostly web OG + landing page + maybe a Product Hunt launch graphic; AI-wrapper = web OG critical (most discovery is shares).
format_options: **Apple App Store Connect (2026)**: iPhone 6.9' (15 Pro Max / 16 Pro Max) — 1320x2868 portrait. iPhone 6.7' (14 Pro Max, 15 Plus, 16 Plus) — 1290x2796 portrait. iPhone 6.5' (11 Pro Max, XS Max) — 1284x2778. iPhone 5.5' (8 Plus / older) — 1242x2208. **As of 2026, only ONE size is required (6.9' OR 6.5'); App Store auto-scales for smaller devices.** Up to 10 screenshots per device per locale. PNG (preferred) or JPEG, no alpha. Optional app preview videos: same dimensions, .mov or .mp4, 15-30 seconds, max 500MB. App preview poster (frame): same aspect, single image. **Google Play (2026)**: phone screenshots min 320px, max 3840px, aspect ratio between 16:9 and 9:16; recommended 1080x1920+. 7-inch tablet 1024x600+. 10-inch tablet 1280x800+. Min 2 screenshots, max 8 (recommend 4-6). Feature graphic: **1024x500 px**, JPG or 24-bit PNG, NO alpha — mandatory. App icon: 512x512 PNG with alpha. Promo video: YouTube URL only (not uploaded). **Web/social OG**: 1200x630 (Open Graph default), 1200x627 (LinkedIn), 1200x675 (Twitter summary_large_image), 1080x1080 (Instagram square), 1080x1920 (Instagram/TikTok story). Pros/cons: PNG for sharp UI screens with text; JPG for photographic OG images (smaller). Apple preview video adds 5-15% conversion lift but costs significant production time.
file_size_performance: App Store screenshots: PNG, target under 500KB each (App Store hard cap is generous but smaller = faster review). Feature graphic: under 1MB. App preview video: under 50MB practical (cap 500MB). OG image: target under 300KB JPG, 600KB PNG; some scrapers (LinkedIn) reject >5MB. Optimize with ImageOptim, oxipng, or `cwebp -q 85` for web only (App Store doesn't accept WebP).
free_tools: 1. **AppMockUp Studio FREE** (https://app-mockup.com) — drag-drop screenshots into device mockups; free templates cover both stores; export at correct dimensions. 2. **AppMockup AI Generator (3 free credits)** (https://appmockupgenerator.com) — Gemini-powered, generates titles + backgrounds + mockups in <2 min. Best for first-time use. 3. **Previewed FREE tier** (https://previewed.app) — straightforward, watermarked free output; good for quick comps. 4. **Mockuuups Studio Free** (https://mockuuups.studio) — 5000+ device mockup templates; free tier limited downloads per month. 5. **Figma Free** + Apple Design Resources templates — the most flexible path; Apple ships free Figma templates for iPhone screen sizes. 6. **Screeny (Figma plugin, FREE)** — App Store + Play Store screenshot builder inside Figma. 7. **AppLaunchpad** — free tier for screenshot generation. 8. **Cleanmock, Smartmockups** — free tier device mockups. 9. **OG Image Playground (Vercel, FREE)** (https://og-playground.vercel.app) — code-driven OG image generation via @vercel/og; the best modern OG approach for web. 10. **Open Graph Image as a Service (Vercel)** — free, generates OG images dynamically per page. 11. **Canva Free** — pre-built OG and social templates. 12. **Bannerbear free trial / GIMP / Photopea** — free Photoshop-equivalent web app. 13. **Figma Plugin: Mockup** — drop screen into device frame inside Figma. 14. **Google Imagen 4 (Gemini Pro)** for hero backgrounds and illustration in screenshot frames.
paid_tools: **Starter ($10-20/mo)**: AppMockUp Pro (~$9/mo) for unlimited generations. Mockuuups Studio Pro ($14/mo) — large mockup library. Previewed paid tier (~$10/mo). **Pro ($20-30/mo)**: Figma Professional ($15/mo) only if collaborating. Rotato ($25/mo) for animated 3D device mockups in app preview videos. ScreenshotBuilder ($29 one-time). **Enterprise ($50+)**: Hire freelance ASO specialist + designer for full first launch ($800-3000 one-time). Mobbin Pro ($24/mo) for design pattern reference (not asset gen but accelerates ideation). **Verdict for our user**: AppMockUp's 3 free credits + Figma Free + Vercel @vercel/og + Gemini Pro covers full first launch. Upgrade to AppMockUp Pro ($9/mo) only if shipping multiple apps quarterly.
gemini_pro_workflow:
**Step 1 — Strategy with Gemini 2.5 Pro chat**: 'Help me write the screenshot narrative for my app. Each of 6 screenshots needs: top-third headline (max 6 words), middle-third visual hook, bottom-third app screenshot. The narrative across 6 screens must follow Problem → Solution → Outcome arc. Brand archetype is {Magician/Creator + Caregiver}. App is {description}. Output 6 panels with headline + visual concept.'

**Step 2 — Imagen 4 for hero backgrounds**: 'Marketing hero background for an app store screenshot, abstract editorial illustration, color palette {brand hex}, balanced negative space for foreground UI overlay, mood {warm/precise/playful}, no gradient mesh, no AI-default pastel, 1320x2868 portrait, illustration quality of {Tracie Ching / Yukai Du}.' Generate 20-30, pick 1 per panel.

**Step 3 — Whisk for style transfer (until 2026-04-30, then Flow)**: Use a real reference (a Linear screenshot, a Headspace screenshot) as Style; your concept sketch as Subject; get matched-style backgrounds.

**Step 4 — App preview video with Veo 3**: Veo 3 (in Gemini Pro) generates short 4-8s clips. Use for transition shots between UI captures (e.g., 'a cursor moves smoothly across a calm gradient background, no UI'). Splice with QuickTime + actual screen recording from iOS Simulator.

**Step 5 — Build a Gem ('Screenshot Designer')**: System prompt: 'You design App Store screenshot frames. Given a UI capture and a panel headline, suggest: layout (top/center/bottom UI placement), background color/illustration, headline typography (size, weight, color), visual hook (icon, arrow, badge). Always: clear hierarchy, readable at thumbnail, archetype-aligned with {Magician/Caregiver}.'

**Step 6 — NotebookLM for ASO research**: Drop in 30 top-ranked competitor app pages (export their screenshots) + the App Store Connect guidelines + 'Smart Outline' the best practices into your own brief.

**Step 7 — Existing master prompt**: User has an `appstore-screenshots-prompt.md` — feed it as the seed system prompt to a Gem, then iterate on it through chat.
workflow_steps: 1. **Audit competitors (60 min)**: Open App Store / Play Store on a desktop browser; capture top-3 competitor screenshots; note their narrative arc. 2. **Write narrative arc (60 min)**: 6-panel headline structure (Problem → Solution → Outcome). Lock headlines first, before any visuals. 3. **Capture clean UI screens (90 min)**: iOS Simulator (Cmd+S to save); ensure status bar shows 9:41, full battery, no notifications, no PII. Capture each panel's hero screen. 4. **Design panel template in Figma (90 min)**: 1320x2868 frame; lock typography (headline font, weight, size, color), background placement, UI screen placement, brand color zones. 5. **Generate hero backgrounds via Imagen 4 (90 min)**: per-panel illustration backgrounds. 6. **Compose all 6 panels in Figma (3 hours)**: place UI screen + headline + background per template. 7. **Export at every required size**: 6.9', 6.5', 6.7' for iPhone; phone + 7-inch + 10-inch for Android. 8. **Build Google Play feature graphic (60 min)**: 1024x500 separate composition — usually app icon + hero illustration + 1-line tagline. 9. **App preview video (4-6 hours, optional)**: capture screen recording in Simulator; edit in iMovie/CapCut; add intro/outro slate; export at 1080x1920. 10. **OG image** (45 min): 1200x630 with hero illustration + headline + brand mark; deploy via @vercel/og dynamic generation for per-page customization. 11. **Localize (variable)**: each locale = full screenshot set re-rendered with translated headlines. **Total**: 12-20 hours first launch; 4-6 hours per refresh.
ai_prompt_templates:
**Template 1 — Screenshot panel hero background**: 'Marketing background for an iOS App Store screenshot panel, abstract editorial illustration, color palette {hex_a, hex_b, hex_c}, balanced negative space across the upper third for headline text, brand archetype {Magician/Creator + Caregiver}, mood {warm/precise/inviting}, no gradient mesh, no AI-default purple pastel, no isometric voxel, no 3D blob, illustration quality referencing {illustrator name}, 1320x2868 portrait, single coherent scene.'

**Template 2 — Google Play feature graphic**: 'Wide horizontal banner 1024x500, hero composition with app icon prominently on left third, brand illustration filling middle, single tagline placeholder zone on right third, color palette {hex tokens}, archetype-aligned, no AI-default sparkles, no purple gradient mesh, JPG-friendly (no transparency).'

**Template 3 — Open Graph share image**: '1200x630 social share image, app name in large {brand font} on the left half, hero illustration on the right half, brand color zone at bottom 80px tall containing app URL, color palette {hex}, designed to read at thumbnail size in Slack/iMessage, no AI gradient mesh, no isometric voxel.'

**Template 4 — App Preview video shot list (Veo prompt)**: 'Short 4-second clip, camera slowly pushes into a {scene description}, color palette {brand}, mood {brand mood}, no UI elements (will composite later), high quality cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical.'

**Template 5 — Headline copy (Gemini text)**: 'Write 6 App Store screenshot headlines for {app name}, each ≤6 words, archetype {Magician/Caregiver}, narrative arc Problem → Solution → Outcome → Social Proof → Differentiation → Call to Action. Avoid: prohibited words like #1, Best, Top, Install Now (Apple/Google reject these).'
claude_code_integration: Drop static screenshot PNGs in `apps/web/public/og/` for direct serving. For dynamic OG images use **@vercel/og** (Edge runtime, free on Vercel hosting): `apps/web/app/og/route.tsx` returns `new ImageResponse(<MyOGComponent>, { width: 1200, height: 630 })`. Claude Code can scaffold the route + JSX. Add per-page OG metadata via Next.js metadata API: `export const metadata = { openGraph: { images: [...] }, twitter: { card: 'summary_large_image', images: [...] } }`. For App Store / Play Store assets, store under `apps/mobile/.fastlane/screenshots/` (Fastlane convention) and submit via `fastlane deliver` — Claude Code can write the Fastfile. For automated screenshot capture, use `expo-screen-capture` or Detox + tests that drive the app to specific states then call `await device.takeScreenshot()` per state. Store the master Figma at `design/marketing/screenshots.fig` (URL in repo README). Validation script: a small Node script that walks `apps/mobile/.fastlane/screenshots/` and verifies each PNG matches the required dimensions before fastlane upload.
emotional_design_integration: **Magician/Creator** screenshots: depth, ambient illustration, type that breathes, single hero visual per panel; the screenshot looks more like a Pentagram poster than a sales pitch. **Caregiver**: warm color palettes (peach, cream, sage), faces or hands visible, gentle headlines ('Take a deep breath'), low visual density. **Jester**: contrarian headlines ('Yes, another habit app'), self-aware visual jokes, breaking the convention (e.g., a screenshot that's mostly empty space with one wink). **Hero**: bold high-contrast headlines, ascending visual motion, before/after framing, social proof prominent. **Outlaw**: dark background, sharp typography, aggressive/anti-establishment tone, leather/grit palette. **Innocent**: bright clean white background, single primary action, friendly mascot pointing. **Sage**: editorial design, generous typography, classical hierarchy, subtle data visualization. **Lover**: warm pinks, candle-light palette, two-people illustrations, intimate captions. For our user's mix: caregiver palette (warm) + magician depth (one ambient illustration per panel) + jester headline edge (one panel breaks the rules — e.g., 'No streaks. No shame.') = unique brand fingerprint that competitors can't replicate.
specific_examples: 1. **Headspace App Store screenshots** (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/headspace-meditation-sleep/id493145008) — Caregiver archetype; warm hand-drawn backgrounds, soft typography, faces-and-bodies illustrations; consistent across all 8 panels. 2. **Linear App Store / web** (https://linear.app) — Magician/Sage; near-monochrome screenshots with single accent color, restrained typography, the 'we're too cool for sales pitch' aesthetic that paradoxically converts. 3. **Duolingo App Store** — Jester; manic Duo expressions, bright contrasting palette, headlines with personality ('Learn for free, forever'). 4. **Cash App** — Outlaw; dark backgrounds, sharp green accent, anti-bank aesthetic. 5. **Notion** — Sage/Creator; minimalist screenshots, generous whitespace, type-forward (text > images). 6. **Things 3 by Cultured Code** — masterclass in Magician archetype; the screenshots feel like editorial design. 7. **Stripe.com web hero + OG** (https://stripe.com) — Hero archetype; restrained, technical confidence, custom hand-illustrated background. 8. **Vercel.com landing** (https://vercel.com) — Magician + Sage; the geometric grid hero became the SaaS-aesthetic-of-2024-2026 (and is now slightly slop-adjacent — be careful). 9. **Apple's own apps screenshots (App Store)** — the gold standard reference; Apple uses real device-grade UI captures, not heavy frames. **References for OG images**: vercel.com, linear.app, raycast.com, stripe.com (open graph in viewSource).
anti_ai_slop_traps: **Tells specific to AI-generated screenshot/marketing assets**: (a) Stock-photo-flavored hero illustrations (smiling diverse team, arms-crossed CEO at laptop). (b) Generic gradient mesh background filling every panel — purple-cyan smoothie. (c) AI-generated app UI mockups instead of real screen captures — ALWAYS use real Simulator screenshots, never AI-generated 'fake' UI; users notice immediately. (d) Six-fingered hands or melted features in any people illustrations. (e) Pastel-mauve-and-mint color schemes default. (f) Headlines that sound AI-written ('Revolutionize your workflow', 'Unlock the power of...'). (g) Tiny floating UI cards in space without context. (h) Identical compositional layout across all 6 panels (AI doesn't vary). (i) Confetti burst on every panel (AI's 'celebration' default). **Marketing-specific slop signatures**: (j) Using one of the 5 Storyset families as the background — Bro, Pana, Amico, Rafiki, Cuate; designers spot in 1 second. (k) Vercel-grid copy aesthetic — black background, geometric mesh, neon accents — was distinctive in 2023, now slop-tier in 2026. (l) The over-used '3D iPhone tilted at 30 degrees' device mockup. **Counter-prompts**: 'editorial illustration', 'real UI capture from iOS Simulator', 'no stock-photo style', 'no smiling-team illustration', 'no Vercel-grid aesthetic', '{specific illustrator reference}'. **Counter-process**: never AI-generate the UI itself — capture real Simulator screens; the human-judging-eye for an authentic app screenshot vs synthetic is brutal. Headlines: write them yourself or have Claude rewrite with explicit anti-AI-cliché brief. Test panel #1 at thumbnail (40x60px) before committing — does it survive?
common_pitfalls: Submitting screenshots with PII (real notification text, email previews, account names). Using a different language in screenshots than the locale's primary language (auto-rejection in some markets). Including screenshot text that violates Apple/Google rules — 'Best app', 'Free', 'Top' all banned. Forgetting to update screenshots after a UI redesign — looks dishonest at install. Mixing aspect ratios — Apple wants strict portrait or strict landscape per panel. Not localizing — leaving English screenshots in DE/JP/FR market = leaving conversion on the table. Optimizing the icon but not screenshots — the icon is a tiny fraction of the conversion pie compared to screenshots #1-3. Submitting low-res — the App Store accepts but it shows. Skipping app preview video — adds 5-15% conversion lift. Forgetting OG images on the web — every shared link looks broken; this is THE highest-leverage marketing fix for any solo dev. Not using @vercel/og or static OG — manually exporting OG images is a maintenance burden; dynamic generation is the modern path.
accessibility_notes: Text in screenshot headlines must be high contrast (4.5:1 minimum, ideally 7:1) — Apple's OCR reads them and surfaces in search; low contrast text is invisible to users with low vision AND hurts ASO. Don't use color-only signals in marketing assets. App preview videos should have captions baked-in (no system captions on App Store). For web OG images, the headline must be readable at thumbnail size (60% of users see OG via mobile preview at ~320x168 effective resolution). Provide alt text for OG images (`og:image:alt`). For animated app preview, ensure no flashing under 3Hz. Screen reader users will hear the App Store description, not see screenshots — make sure description text is independent and standalone.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile (App Store + Play Store): strict aspect ratios, PNG/JPG only, no transparency, dimensions tied to specific iPhone/Android device sizes, max file sizes generous but recommended <500KB-1MB. Submission flow via App Store Connect web UI or Fastlane CLI; Play Store via Play Console. Web (OG/Twitter/LinkedIn): more flexible, 1200x630 standard, 600KB-2MB acceptable, but scrapers cache aggressively (use ?v=2 query string on URL changes). Web supports dynamic OG generation per-page (@vercel/og, satori) — mobile doesn't. Mobile screenshot text is read by Apple OCR for search; web OG text is read by social platforms for thumbnail layout. Localization: App Store / Play Store re-uploads per locale; web OG can server-render per Accept-Language.
